concerns.analysis:
Multiple meaningful charts are completely missing from the HTML output and have been replaced by bracketed text placeholders. For example, the line charts for Figures 14-18 and 21-23 in the source PDF contain critical data visualizations (such as projected spawning stock biomass). In the HTML, these images are absent and instead represented as text paragraphs containing the alt text (e.g., `

[Image: Line chart showing projections of Spawning Stock Biomass...]

`).
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Figure 14: Projections of SSB related to fishing at a level to end overfishing... line chart showing data from 2018 to 2050.
concerns.output_quote: [Image: Line chart showing projections of Spawning Stock Biomass (SSB) from 2018 to 2050 under different fishing scenarios...]

concerns.analysis: The document contains multiple nested lists. While most nested lists (like the identical text on page 24) were extracted correctly, the list on page 14 suffers from hierarchy flattening. The sub-items under the bullet 'From Supplement A to the Southern Flounder FMP Amendment 1...' are visually indented in the source PDF to indicate they are sub-bullets. However, in the JSON output, they are rendered as top-level sibling items in the 'items' array rather than being properly nested inside a 'child
concerns.location: Page 14, list under 'Amendment 1 Management Carried Forward in Amendment 2'

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 2 table(s) with repeated headers across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=2. Examples: pages 47-48: 5 cols (issue, ncdmf, southern flounder advisory committee...); pages 48-49: 5 cols (issue, ncdmf, southern flounder advisory committee...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements with duplicated header rows. The HTML output will show multiple tables instead of one continuous table — a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Output has more images than source PDF (ratio 1.8x) — possible image duplication
concerns.analysis: image_count_ratio=1.77. More images in output than in source indicates duplicated or fabricated image elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 6 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=6, examples=['https://www.implan.com', 'http://www.implan.com/', 'mailto:Michael.loeffler@ncdenr.gov']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "Year"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=1. Tables use <th> for data values (misrepresenting data as headers) or have a data row mismarked as a header row. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.
header_footer_count=61. Header/footer elements are present in extraction data. This is common in multi-page government PDFs and is typically cosmetic — the renderer strips them from HTML output.
